body.title: CLOSED: Librarian focus quality log
body.result: focus_quality_log DDL + read_quality_log() prune_candidate stats; 5 new tests; 14 total green
body.description:
Schema: `instance.focus_quality_log` — id serial PK, ne_cycle_ts timestamptz, memory_id bigint, was_loaded bool, was_used bool, contribution_score float DEFAULT 0.0, logged_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(). After each NE cycle, record which memory IDs were loaded as context and whether they appeared in the NE output (was_used). The Librarian reads focus_quality_log to weight prune recommendations: memories repeatedly loaded but never used are highest-value prune candidates.

API in Librarian curation_tools: read_quality_log(memory_id, days=30) → contribution stats. NE logs via direct psycopg2 INSERT (or via a new proposals mechanism — whichever is simpler given the sprint state at implementation time).

**Affected files:** `agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/tools/curation_tools.py` (read_quality_log), DB migration (CREATE TABLE instance.focus_quality_log)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; direct psycopg2; append-only; no cortex.py touch
**Scope boundary:** IN — focus_quality_log schema, Librarian read path. OUT — NE write path (separate or part of grader work), no auto-prune from quality log alone.
**Test plan:** seeded focus_quality_log rows; curation_tools identifies low-contribution-score memories as prune candidates; high-contribution memories skipped
